summary:
The latest technique that allows obtaining a bone graft without damaging vital structures like blood vessels or nerves involves using piezosurgery. This method employs focused ultrasonic waves that selectively vibrate bone because it has different density than surrounding soft tissues, thereby preserving adjacent vascular and neural components intact.

Okay, I need to summarize Agent1’s reasoning strictly within two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant aspects related to the query—specifically how this new approach prevents harm to essential anatomical features such as vessels/nerves when extracting bone graft material.

The summary must highlight why piezosurgery meets these criteria over other methods considered earlier—the core advantage lies in its ability to distinguish bones from soft tissues via physical properties enabling selective cutting under minimal risk to delicate surroundings. 

I will ensure all content remains grounded exclusively in direct evidence provided by the original response regarding safety mechanisms tied to actual surgical outcomes.
